But wait - I need to check if the shape itself matches, not just the red block position. Let me look at the current state and also check what it looked like after 1 SPACE vs the green shape.

Let me check the current white shape to see its orientation, and compare with the green shape.

    print(f"Row {r}: {line}")
</parameter>
</function>
</tool_call>

Actually, I should just try one more SPACE to get to 270° rotation (red blocks on right), and then another to get back to original. The cycle is 4 states.

So the shape is rotating. After 3 more SPACE presses, it should cycle back.

Maybe the goal is to rotate the white shape until it matches the green shape's orientation. Since the green shape has the "opening" (or hole) in a vertical orientation, I need the white shape to also have a vertical hole.
